tokens.\\binom{n}{k}: Combinations: Ways to choose k successes from n trials.
tokens.p^k: Probability of obtaining exactly k successes.
tokens.(1-p)^{n-k}: Probability of obtaining exactly n-k failures.

tokens.\\lambda^k: The average rate of success (λ) raised to the power of the target occurrences (k).
tokens.e^{-\\lambda}: Euler's number (e ≈ 2.718) raised to the negative average rate.
tokens.k!: The factorial of the target number of occurrences.

tokens.\\frac{1}{\\sigma \\sqrt{2\\pi}}: The normalization constant ensuring the total area under the curve is 1.
tokens.e^{-\\frac{1}{2}\\left(\\frac{x-\\mu}{\\: The exponential component that creates the symmetric bell curve shape.
tokens.\\mu: The mean (expected value) around which the distribution is centered.
tokens.\\sigma: The standard deviation, controlling the spread or width of the curve.

tokens.{\\lambda}: The rate parameter (events per unit of time/space).
tokens.e^{-\\lambda x}: The exponential decay representing the probability of no event occurring until time x.

tokens.{b}: The maximum possible value in the distribution's range.
tokens.{a}: The minimum possible value in the distribution's range.

tokens.{p}: The probability of success on a single trial.
tokens.(1-p)^{k-1}: The probability of encountering exactly k-1 failures before the first success.

tokens.\\sum_{i=1}^{N} x_i: The sum of all values in the population.
tokens.N: The total number of items in the population.

tokens.\\sum_{i=1}^{n} x_i: The sum of all values in the sample.
tokens.n: The total number of items in the sample.

tokens.(x_i - \\mu)^2: The squared difference between each value and the population mean.
tokens.N: The total number of items in the population.

tokens.(x_i - \\bar{x})^2: The squared difference between each value and the sample mean.
tokens.n - 1: Bessel's correction (degrees of freedom) for unbiased estimation.

tokens.\\sigma: The standard deviation, which is the square root of the variance.

tokens.P(A|B): The posterior probability: probability of A given that B is true.
tokens.P(B|A): The likelihood: probability of observing B given that A is true.
tokens.P(B): The marginal probability (total probability) of observing B.

tokens.C(n, r): The number of combinations (order does not matter).
tokens.n!: Factorial of the total number of items.
tokens.r!: Factorial of the chosen number of items.

tokens.n!: Factorial of the total number of items.

tokens.\\frac{1}{2^{k/2} \\Gamma(k/2)}: The normalization constant involving the Gamma function.
tokens.x^{k/2 - 1}: The shape element of the distribution depending on degrees of freedom (k).

tokens.e^{-(x/\\lambda)^k}: The exponential decay representing survival probability.

tokens.d_1, d_2: Degrees of freedom for the numerator and denominator.
